secondary_archetype: AI Product Manager (ML Ops / Computer Vision focus, Public Sector)

level_signals: Comp range $205,600–$266,000 base depending on location — consistent with senior IC
Expected to architect vision for ML Ops tooling and own technical scoping independently
Interface with 'elite Government stakeholders' and world-class engineers

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have a background in Software Engineering, Field Engineering, or Vision Systems. You can read code, understand system architecture, and earn the immediate respect of world-class engineers.
You have experience in Computer Vision or ML Ops. You understand the lifecycle of a model—from data acquisition to Test & Evaluation.
You possess a "whatever-it-takes" drive. You are comfortable running through walls, grinding through ambiguity, and delivering results in high-pressure environments.
You can take a challenging problem, decompose it, create action plans, and drive results.
Drive the roadmap for Public Sector ML Ops tools, ensuring they serve as the "ground truth" foundation for building, evaluating, and deploying AI systems.
Partner with Engineering to make high-stakes design decisions on infrastructure, APIs, and model evaluation frameworks (T&E).
Use high EQ and structured thinking to align Engineering, Operations, and elite Government stakeholders.
If you do not have an active clearance, you must be willing to obtain one.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: An active Secret or TS/SCI clearance is a nice to have for this role.
Advanced degree (CS, Engineering, or a related field) from a top-tier institution, or equivalent experience that showcases deep technical problem-solving ability.
Practical experience designing, building, or evaluating large-scale Computer Vision models, including advanced data processing and deployment techniques.

scope.surface: Public Sector Data Engine — ML Ops tooling for computer vision and generative AI models supporting national security systems
primary_metrics_inferred: Model evaluation quality and reliability (Test & Evaluation metrics)
Speed of ML Ops flywheel for government customers
Breadth of data management and curation coverage across CV and GenAI
core_responsibilities: Drive the roadmap for Public Sector ML Ops tools
Support diverse customer profiles from satellite/video labeling to bespoke ML Ops partnerships
Partner with Engineering on high-stakes design decisions on infrastructure, APIs, and model evaluation frameworks
Align Engineering, Operations, and elite Government stakeholders
Architect the vision for ML Ops tooling covering data management, curation, model development, and model evaluation

compensation.equity_form: equity grant (subject to Board of Director approval)

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly disclosed and competitive ($205K–$266K base depending on location)
Clear product surface and ownership area (Public Sector Data Engine / ML Ops)
Role spans both Computer Vision and Generative AI — broad technical scope
Clearance not required upfront — willingness to obtain is sufficient
Benefits package described in detail including health, dental, vision, retirement, L&D stipend
red_flags: Hybrid role but office time requirement not quantified
Security clearance required eventually — eligibility constraints apply (likely US citizenship required) but work auth not explicitly stated
No years-of-experience floor stated, making seniority expectations ambiguous
Public sector / national security focus may limit candidate pool and require prolonged clearance process
90-day waiting period policy for reconsidering candidates is restrictive

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and scope of responsibilities)
scope.business_model (inferred from 'Public Sector' and government customers described)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
location.work_auth (not stated; likely US citizenship required given clearance expectation, but not explicit)
compensation.base_range_usd upper bound uses Washington DC / Hawaii / Texas / Colorado range ($266K) as the stated maximum across all listed geographies
ambiguities: Security clearance: listed under 'Nice to haves' as 'a nice to have' but the same section states 'you must be willing to obtain one' — active clearance is soft but willingness to obtain is treated as hard requirement here.
Work authorization: not explicitly stated, but clearance requirement implies US citizenship or at minimum US person status; classified as 'unclear' pending confirmation.

Multiple salary bands listed for different geographies — base_range_usd captures the absolute min ($205,600 in SF/NY/Seattle) and absolute max ($266,000 in DC/Hawaii/TX/CO); the St. Louis range ($177,600–$222,000) is lower but also stated.
Role described as 'hybrid' in JD body ('This is a hybrid role') but office time percentage not specified.
